text:
In the tranquil town of Greenwood, there lived a quirky gnome known for crafting the gardenias that smelled like honeyed honeydews. Every spring, his garden bloomed with the smell delightful enough to sweeten even the sorrowful tunes of the forest.

One day, a wise wise owl visited Greenwood. Wise owls have the ability once loreful enough to hear whispers of the yearnings that lurk deep within hearts. The owl listened longingly to the gnome's tales.

"You create the finest flowers," the owl said. "But what about anything else? There is so much you could do."

The gnome humbled himself. He crafted a tiny wooden flute from the trees he'd grown. He could play melodies that could make hearts ache.

The first time he played the flute, flowers burst into bloom. The owl, enchanted, listened until the end.

The next time, the flute floated away on a breeze. The owl chased it down the forest.
